message.content:
You are producing TWO ranked shortlists from the convertible set of Coachella Valley dining establishments.

Convertible = independently owned, reachable local decision-maker, evidence of existing marketing spend (real site, paid listings, agency footprint, active social).

Of the 80 assessed entities, 49 are tagged convertible. Below are their full assessments + verification verdicts.

SHORTLIST 1 — BEST DEMO CANDIDATES (target ~10):
Most dramatic, legible reputation-to-visibility gap. The ones that make an outreach report sing. High reputation + low visibility = strong demo. Articulate the gap concretely.

SHORTLIST 2 — BEST REVENUE CANDIDATES (target ~10):
Highest probability of converting. Accessible decision-maker, real marketing budget evidence, genuine pain. May overlap with demo list — surface the overlap.

WEIGHTING: bias toward mid-valley (Rancho Mirage, Palm Desert) for outreach priority. Keep at least 60% of each shortlist in mid-valley if candidates support it.

For each candidate:
- name, city
- demo list: reputation summary, visibility summary, gap articulation, why_demo_makes_a_report_sing
- revenue list: ownership signal, marketing spend evidence, accessibility read, revenue rationale

End with mid_valley_weighting_note explaining how the weighting played out.

Convertible assessments:
[
  {
    "name": "Wally's Desert Turtle",
    "city": "Rancho Mirage",
    "cuisine_or_type": "Continental / California fine dining (classic, soufflé-forward, jacket-era room)",
    "corridor": "Highway 111 corridor, Rancho Mirage (the original Rancho Mirage 'restaurant row' anchor, near The River)",
    "reputation": {
      "review_volume": "Yelp 515 reviews; Tripadvisor 357 reviews (#3 of 80 in Rancho Mirage); OpenTable cited as 4.6 across ~4,266 diner reviews. Google review count [UNVERIFIED] — not directly fetched.",
      "sentiment": "Mostly positive — strong recurring praise for service, nostalgia/Rat Pack ambiance, Dover sole, rack of lamb, and soufflés; minority of reviews note high prices and occasional pacing/wait issues.",
      "recency_signal": "Recent 2025-2026 reviews continue to praise service polish, curated menus, live music in the Sahara Lounge, and the third-generation refresh under Madalyn Botello; restaurant is seasonal and reopens October 9, 2026.",
      "longevity": "Operating since 1978 — 48 years. Continuous family ownership: founder Wally Botello, then son Michael Botello (after Wally's passing in 1985), and now third-generation owner Madalyn Botello (Cornell hospitality alumna).",
      "credentials": [
        "Wine Spectator Award of Excellence (current as of 2025)",
        "AAA Four Diamond Award (long-running; site/press claim continuous since 1993 — [UNVERIFIED] for the start-year specifically)",
        "DiRoNA Award (Distinguished Restaurants of North America) — cited in press [UNVERIFIED] for current status",
        "Mobil Travel Four Star Award — cited in press [UNVERIFIED] for current/historic status",
        "Featured on Palm Springs Life 'oldest restaurants' / 'legends of fine dining' lists",
        "Featured on Visit Greater Palm Springs and OpenTable historic-restaurants lists"
      ]
    },
    "agent_visibility": {
      "appears_in_queries": "Surfaces strongly for: 'best fine dining Rancho Mirage', 'romantic / date night Rancho Mirage', 'historic restaurants Greater Palm Springs', 'continental restaurants Palm Springs / Palm Desert cities'. Does NOT reliably surface for breakfast queries (dinner-only, seasonal), and is overshadowed by Melvyn's, Le Vallauris, and Johannes on broad 'continental Palm Springs' queries that center Palm Springs proper.",
      "description_accuracy": "Generally accurate when surfaced — 1978 founding, Botello family, soufflés, Dover sole, rack of lamb, Rancho Mirage location, fine dining/Rat Pack tone are correctly attributed. Some aggregator listings mis-categorize as 'Steakhouse' (Yelp) or 'American' rather than Continental/California, which understates the cuisine signal.",
      "site_structured_data": "Site (Squarespace) presents clean human-readable hours, menu sections (Dinner, Desserts, Bar & Happy Hour, Wine, Restaurant Week, Private Dining), address, phone, and social links. JSON-LD / schema.org Restaurant markup was not confirmed in fetch — agent-readable structured data is [UNVERIFIED] and likely thin beyond Squarespace defaults. Seasonal-closure messaging is prominent but not expressed as machine-readable openingHoursSpecification."
    },
    "visibility_gap_score": 4,
    "relationships": {
      "competitors": [
        "The Edge Steakhouse (Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage)",
        "Catalan Mediterranean Cuisine (Rancho Mirage)",
        "Willie's Modern Fare (Rancho Mirage)",
        "Le Vallauris (Palm Springs) — cross-city continental peer",
        "Melvyn's at the Ingleside Inn (Palm Springs) — cross-city continental peer"
      ],
      "corridor_role": "anchor — one of the founding fine-dining anchors of the Highway 111 / Rancho Mirage 'restaurant row' corridor; cited as 'pioneer of fine dining in the desert' in regional press.",
      "query_wins": [
        "historic / oldest restaurants Coachella Valley",
        "fine dining Rancho Mirage",
        "romantic / date night Rancho Mirage",
        "Greater Palm Springs Restaurant Week (Rancho Mirage participant)",
        "soufflé / Dover sole desert"
      ]
    },
    "convertibility": {
      "tier": "convertible",
      "ownership_signal": "independent — third-generation family-owned (Botello family); not chain, not resort-owned, not hotel-attached.",
      "marketing_spend_signal": "Custom Squarespace site with dedicated Press, Events, and Restaurant Week 2026 pages; active Instagram (@wallysdesertturtle) and Facebook; OpenTable presence with ~4,266 diner reviews indicating sustained paid-listing/reservation-channel investment; Wine Spectator Award of Excellence (paid submission program); ongoing live-music / Sahara Lounge programming that requires marketing cadence; recurring Palm Springs Life editorial placement.",
      "reasoning": "Wally's is a textbook convertible target: independently owned by an identifiable, reachable decision-maker (Madalyn Botello, third-generation owner-operator with a hospitality-school background and a public LinkedIn presence), with clear and sustained evidence of marketing spend — custom website, multi-platform paid reservation/listing channels, active social, award-submission programs, and curated event calendars. Reputation is genuinely strong (48-year longevity, AAA Four Diamond, Wine Spectator, multi-platform 4.3-4.6 sentiment), but agent-readable structured data on the owned site is thin and aggregator cuisine tagging is inconsistent — leaving a real, fixable visibility gap that an owner who already invests in marketing would plausibly act on."
    },
    "sources_consulted": [
      "https://www.wallysdesertturtle.com/",
      "https://www.winespectator.com/restaurants/8325/wallys-desert-turtle",
      "https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurant_Review-g32942-d473525-Reviews-Wally_s_Desert_Turtle-Rancho_Mirage_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html",
      "https://www.yelp.com/biz/wallys-desert-turtle-rancho-mirage",
      "https://www.opentable.com/r/wallys-desert-turtle-rancho-mirage",
      "https://www.palmspringslife.com/the-oldest-restaurants-in-the-desert/",
      "https://www.visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/blog/post/historic-restaurants-in-greater-palm-springs/",
      "https://www.opentable.com/features/historic-restaurants-palm-springs-ca",
      "https://www.opentable.com/features/best-fine-dining-restaurants-palm-springs-palm-desert-cities-ca-rancho-mirage",
      "https://localemagazine.com/rancho-mirage-restaurants/",
      "https://voyagela.com/interview/inspiring-conversations-with-madalyn-botello-of-wallys-desert-turtle/",
      "https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/wallys-desert-turtle-rancho-mirage-california/",
      "https://newsroom.aaa.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/AAA-Four-Diamond-Restaurants-2025-2.pdf"
    ],
    "flags": [
      "[UNVERIFIED] Google Business Profile review count — Yelp page (403 on direct fetch) cited via search snippet at 515 reviews; Google count not directly retrieved.",
      "[UNVERIFIED] AAA Four Diamond start year of 1993 — claim appears in third-party summary; not confirmed on AAA's own current 2025 Four Diamond restaurants list (PDF not parsed).",
      "[UNVERIFIED] DiRoNA Award current status — appears in summary text but not confirmed against the DiRoNA roster.",
      "[UNVERIFIED] Mobil Travel Four Star Award — Mobil Travel Guide rebranded to Forbes Travel Guide in 2009; the 'Mobil Four Star' designation is historical, not current.",
      "[UNVERIFIED] schema.org / JSON-LD structured data on wallysdesertturtle.com — page-level fetch did not confirm Restaurant schema; likely Squarespace default only.",
      "[UNVERIFIED] OpenTable 4.6 / 4,266 diner reviews — cited from search snippet; direct OpenTable fetch timed out."
    ],
    "surface_count": 5,
    "verdicts": [
      {
        "lens": "credentials-and-history",
        "confirms": [
          "Operating since 1978 — verified via multiple primary/secondary sources (Palm Springs Life, Locale Magazine, official site)",
          "Continuous Botello family ownership across three generations: founder Wally Botello, son Michael Botello (after Wally's death in 1985), and granddaughter Madalyn Botello (took over from her father in 2020)",
          "AAA Four Diamond Award current as of 2025 — verified on AAA.com restaurant listing and AAA's 2025 Four Diamond Restaurants PDF",
          "AAA Four Diamond continuous since 1993 — confirmed; AAA's list convention shows the year next to the property as the first year of current consecutively maintained Diamond designation",
          "Wine Spectator Award of Excellence current — verified directly on winespectator.com listing (Award of Excellence, 2025)"
        ],
        "contradicts": [
          "The claim of a current 'Mobil Travel Four Star Award' cannot be current: the Mobil Travel Guide was rebranded to Forbes Travel Guide in 2009, so any 'Mobil Four Star' reference is historic only, not a current credential. The brief already flags this as [UNVERIFIED] for current status; the contradiction is that no current 'Mobil' award exists for any restaurant."
        ],
        "flagged_as_unverified": [
          "DiRoNA Award current status — restaurant and press repeatedly claim it, but I could not locate Wally's Desert Turtle on a current DiRoNA member/award list (DiRoNA find-a-restaurant page returned 404 in this check); claim is plausible but not independently confirmed as current",
          "Madalyn Botello Cornell hospitality alumna — not independently verified in any of the fetched sources (official About page only describes her as third-generation leader)",
          "Mobil Four Star historic status — plausible given era but not independently confirmed via a primary Mobil Travel Guide record"
        ],
        "confidence": "high",
        "notes": "Core history and headline credentials hold up under scrutiny: 1978 founding, three-generation Botello ownership, AAA Four Diamond continuously since 1993, and Wine Spectator Award of Excellence 2025 are all independently verifiable. The weak link is the awards stack repeated verbatim across press/aggregators: 'Mobil Four Star' is necessarily historic (brand retired in 2009), and DiRoNA current status could not be confirmed on DiRoNA's own site within this check — both should be softened or qualified as historic/claimed rather than current. Madalyn Botello's Cornell credential is unverified and should be sourced before publishing."
      },
      {
        "lens": "visibility-and-web-presence",
        "confirms": [
          "Tripadvisor: 357 reviews, ranked #3 of 80 in Rancho Mirage (confirmed via direct fetch of Tripadvisor page; rating 4.3).",
          "Yelp review volume: 515 reviews (confirmed via Yelp listing title 'Updated May 2026 - 468 Photos & 515 Reviews').",
          "OpenTable: 4.6 rating across ~4,266 diner reviews (confirmed via search snippet citing OpenTable directly).",
          "Founded 1978 by Wally Botello (founder of the Velvet Turtle chain); confirmed across multiple sources including localemagazine, Palm Springs Life, and the official site.",
          "Family ownership lineage: Wally (d.1985) -> Michael Botello -> Madalyn Botello (third generation). Confirmed via wallysdesertturtle.com/about and Palm Springs Life.",
          "Madalyn Botello is a Cornell hospitality alumna — Cornell University Hotel School, BS Hospitality, class of 2017. Confirmed via Palm Springs Life feature.",
          "Surfaces strongly on 'best fine dining Rancho Mirage' queries — appears on Yelp top 10 fine dining, OpenTable's '10 Best Fine Dining Restaurants in Rancho Mirage,' Palm Springs Life '6 Rancho Mirage Restaurants Worth a Reservation,' and Locale Magazine 'date night Rancho Mirage' lists.",
          "Sentiment profile (service, Rat Pack/nostalgia, Dover sole, rack of lamb, soufflés) matches what appears in cited reviews; soufflés and service are repeatedly highlighted in OpenTable summary.",
          "Squarespace site lacks JSON-LD / schema.org Restaurant structured data. Independently confirmed via fetch — only plain HTML address, tel: link, and human-readable seasonal-closure text. No openingHoursSpecification, no menu schema."
        ],
        "contradicts": [
          "Seasonal status as currently presented on the homepage: site explicitly says 'CLOSED FOR THE SEASON. We will reopen on October 9, 2026.' This is consistent with the 'seasonal' note in the assessment but stronger — the homepage is currently a closure notice, which materially affects current visibility/convertibility. The brief should reflect that the site at audit time is in closed-for-season state.",
          "Wine Spectator Award of Excellence 'current as of 2025' — site shows a Wine Spectator badge but the about page does not specify award year. The claim is plausible but the year-currency wording overstates what is directly visible on the site."
        ],
        "flagged_as_unverified": [
          "AAA Four Diamond continuous since 1993 — the start-year 1993 is repeated in a third-party AAA.com travel listing (confirms current AAA Four Diamond), but 'continuous since 1993' was not independently corroborated against an AAA historical list (the 2025 PDF could not be parsed).",
          "DiRoNA Award current status — cited in third-party listings (e.g., AAA.com travel page) as a recipient, but DiRoNA's own current membership roster was not independently checked.",
          "Mobil Travel Four Star Award — cited in third-party listing alongside AAA/DiRoNA; Mobil Travel Guide rebranded to Forbes Travel Guide in 2009, so any 'current' Mobil Four Star claim is anachronistic and should be reframed as historic.",
          "Google review count — not fetched (consistent with original [UNVERIFIED] flag).",
          "Palm Springs Life 'oldest restaurants' / 'legends of fine dining' specific list inclusion — Palm Springs Life features on the restaurant are confirmed, but the specific list titles cited were not directly enumerated.",
          "Visit Greater Palm Springs and OpenTable historic-restaurants list inclusion — Visit GPS surfaces Rancho Mirage restaurant directory but specific historic-list membership not directly confirmed."
        ],
        "confidence": "high",
        "notes": "Core review-volume and ranking claims hold up (Yelp 515, Tripadvisor 357 #3 of 80, OpenTable 4.6/~4266). Lineage and Cornell credential for Madalyn Botello verified. Structured-data assessment verified — Squarespace site has no JSON-LD Restaurant markup, only plain HTML. Two cautions for the brief: (1) the homepage is currently displaying a hard 'closed for season, reopen Oct 9, 2026' notice, which is a current-state visibility issue the brief should call out explicitly; (2) the 'Mobil Travel Four Star' credential is anachronistic — Mobil rebranded to Forbes Travel Guide in 2009, so listing 'Mobil' as a present credential is misleading and should be reframed as historic. AAA Four Diamond 'since 1993' could not be independently sourced beyond third-party repetition. Visibility gap score of 4 and 'convertible' tier are reasonable given strong third-party surfacing but thin first-party structured data and seasonal-closure homepage."
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "name": "Workshop Kitchen + Bar",
    "city": "Palm Springs",
    "corridor": "Uptown Design District (N. Palm Canyon Dr.)",
    "cuisine_or_type": "New American / Modern Californian, farm-to-table, wood-fired",

No signal of closure or major decline — restaurant active through 2026.",
      "longevity": "Operating since October 2012 (13+ years); original chef/owner Michael Beckman and co-owner Joseph Mourani still attached per current site — ownership stable",
      "credentials": [
        "James Beard Foundation Award — Outstanding Restaurant Design, 75+ seats (2015, awarded to SOMA Architects for the space)",
        "Michelin Guide recommended — multi-year (described as 4-time Michelin recommended; Michelin Plate 2024 and 2025)",
        "Restaurant & Bar Design Awards — Best Design for the Americas (2013)",

Does not surface for cuisine-specific queries like 'New American near Palm Springs' as consistently as its reputation would suggest — agents often default to Eight4Nine, Trio, and Jake's for that bracket.",
      "description_accuracy": "Generally accurate when it does surface: Michelin-recommended, James Beard design winner, farm-to-table California cuisine, uptown design district address. Some agent summaries conflate 'Michelin Plate / Recommended' with 'Michelin star' or 'consecutive Michelin stars' (Tripadvisor summary above made this error) — a real hallucination risk to watch.",
      "site_structured_data": "Weak. workshopkitchenbar.com presents address, hours, and menu in human-readable text only. No schema.org JSON-LD Restaurant/LocalBusiness markup detected, no clear Open Graph or microdata. Hours wording ('Sunday Brunch Everyday 10am-2pm, 5pm-10pm') is ambiguous for parsers. Resy handles reservations off-site. Strong human design, weak agent-readable signal."

    "visibility_gap_score": 6,

        "Copley's on Palm Canyon (Palm Springs) — historic-home fine dining peer",
        "Eight4Nine (Palm Springs uptown) — refined New American, direct corridor competitor",
        "Trio Palm Springs (uptown design district) — American bistro, same-corridor competitor at a lower price tier"

      "corridor_role": "anchor — Workshop is the design-and-credential anchor of the Uptown Design District restaurant cluster; Trio, Eight4Nine, Birba and Jake's read as supporting venues in the same corridor",
      "query_wins": [

        "'Michelin recommended Palm Springs'",
        "'restaurant design Palm Springs'",

      "marketing_spend_signal": "Active branded site (workshopkitchenbar.com) plus a separate catering site (workshopkitchencatering.com) and a Palm Springs sub-route — suggests in-house or contracted web work. Active Instagram (@workshopkitchenbar), Facebook, X. Paid presence on Resy, OpenTable, Yelp, Tripadvisor. Earned-media footprint in Palm Springs Life, Hotels Above Par, Descanso Resort blog, and regional guides — consistent with a venue that invests in PR. No agency footprint detected, but design-forward brand discipline is evident.",
      "reasoning": "Workshop is an owner-operator independent with credentialed reputation, an active multi-site web presence, paid platform listings, and demonstrable PR activity. Chef/owner Michael Beckman and Joseph Mourani are reachable via the site's posted email and phone. The venue clearly invests in brand and marketing yet ships a website with no schema.org markup, ambiguous hours formatting, and weak machine-readable location/menu signal — a textbook convertible: high reputation, real spend, fixable agent-visibility gap."
    },
    "sources_consulted": [

One source mischaracterized it as 'Michelin stars'; that is almost certainly an error and should not be repeated.",

        ],
        "contradicts": [

        ],
        "flagged_as_unverified": [

          "Time Out Palm Springs feature — not surfaced in independent searches.",
          "James Beard nomination/award for the restaurant or chef directly (beyond the SOMA design award) — none found, and the claim only attributes the award to SOMA, which is correct. No conflicting evidence."
        ],
        "confidence": "high",
        "notes": "Core credentials all verifiable through primary/secondary sources: 2015 JBF Outstanding Restaurant Design (75+ seats) for SOMA Architects, 2013 Restaurant & Bar Design Awards Best Americas, and Michelin Plate 2024–2025. Ownership (Beckman + Mourani) confirmed via current restaurant site. Only material correction: opening date is September 22, 2012, not October 2012 — the '13+ years' longevity claim still holds. The 'multi-year / 4-time Michelin recommended' phrasing should be softened to '2024 and 2025 Michelin Plate (consecutive)' unless additional guide years can be independently sourced. Overall the credentials block is solid and defensible."

      {
        "lens": "visibility-and-web-presence",
        "confirms": [
          "Yelp review count of 1,596 confirmed verbatim on Yelp listing",
          "James Beard Foundation Award 2015 for Outstanding Restaurant Design (76 Seats & Over) — confirmed via PR Newswire and Palm Springs Life; awarded to SOMA Architects/Michel Abboud for the space",
          "Restaurant & Bar Design Awards 2013 'Best Design for the Americas' — confirmed via multiple sources and the restaurant's own About page",
          "Michelin Guide recommended (listed in 2025 USA Michelin Guide; described as '4-time Michelin recommended' in multiple sources)",
          "Ownership stable — Michael Beckman (Chef/Owner) and Joseph Mourani (co-owner) still attached per current website About page; they met at Paul Bocuse Institute and partnered in 2012",

          "Google rating 4.3 and OpenTable rating 4.4 anchors confirmed via aggregated review data"
        ],
        "contradicts": [
          "Claim states 'Operating since October 2012' — multiple sources (including operator history) state Workshop Kitchen + Bar opened September 22, 2012, not October. Minor but factual error.",
          "Claim states website hours wording 'Sunday Brunch Everyday 10am-2pm, 5pm-10pm' — the actual current site wording per fetch is 'Sunday Brunch: 10am-2pm daily' and 'Dinner: 5pm-10pm everyday' (two separate lines). The garbled phrasing in the claim appears to misrepresent the site copy, though both versions are still ambiguous for parsers so the underlying weak-signal conclusion stands."
        ],
        "flagged_as_unverified": [
          "Google 1,250+ reviews — could not independently confirm exact count via search results",
          "OpenTable 1,788 ratings — exact count not surfaced in search results (OpenTable listing exists but specific count not visible)",
          "Tripadvisor 831 reviews — exact count not independently confirmed in search results",
          "Aggregate 5,000+ across platforms — depends on unverified individual platform counts",
          "Sentiment characterization 'split between exceptional and disappointed' on Yelp/Tripadvisor — directional plausibility but not adversarially sampled review-by-review",
          "Specific assertion that 'agents often default to Eight4Nine, Trio, and Jake's' for New American near Palm Springs queries — not directly tested in this run",
          "Visibility gap score of 6 and 'convertible' tier — analyst-derived judgments, not externally verifiable facts",
          "Whether Michelin Plate designation specifically applied in both 2024 and 2025 (Michelin Guide listing confirmed but plate-vs-recommended-by-year not granularly verified)"
        ],
        "confidence": "high",
        "notes": "Core credentials (James Beard 2015, R&B Design Awards 2013, Michelin recommendation, ownership continuity, weak structured-data signal) all hold up cleanly under independent verification. Two factual nits: opening month is September 2012 not October, and the site hours quote is slightly garbled in the claim. The visibility-pattern intuition — strong on Michelin/James Beard/design-district queries, weak on Coachella-Valley-wide rollups — matches what I see in live SERPs.         "Sabatinos / Your Pie / TKB Bakery (Indio-area Italian/pizza alternatives surfacing alongside Ciro's)"
      ],
      "corridor_role": "anchor (multi-decade independent on the Hwy 111 Indio dining strip; one of the oldest continuously operating sit-down restaurants in the eastern valley)",
      "query_wins": [
        "best Italian food in Indio",
        "Italian restaurant Indio Hwy 111",
        "favorite Italian Coachella Valley (self-claimed)",
        "old-school / classic Italian Coachella Valley",
        "early bird Italian Indio"
      ]
    },
    "convertibility": {
      "tier": "convertible",
      "ownership_signal": "independent",
      "marketing_spend_signal": "Owns a branded domain (cirosindio.com) — though it appears lightly maintained / bot-blocked. Active on Facebook (two separate pages: 'Ciro's Pizza Indio' and 'Ciros of Indio') suggesting page proliferation rather than managed social. Present on paid/free aggregators (Grubhub, Seamless, UberEats, OpenTable x2, SinglePlatform menu syndication, Yelp claimed) but no agency footprint visible, no PR/press placements in Palm Springs Life or Desert Sun surfacing in search, and no evidence of structured-data investment. Marketing spend appears low and reactive rather than strategic.",
      "reasoning": "Independent family-operated restaurant (Hare family carrying on Mazzella tradition) with a reachable local decision-maker, 60-year continuous operation, and demonstrated willingness to invest in basic digital presence (own domain, multiple delivery integrations). The visibility gap is large: strong word-of-mouth reputation and a defensible 'favorite Italian / favorite pizza' anchor claim are not converting into top-of-list placement on pizza and date-night queries where newer competitors (Piero's, Italica) dominate, and an erroneous 'CLOSED' flag on at least one aggregator plus duplicate OpenTable listings are actively eroding agent trust. Clean structured data, consolidated listings, and a press/awards refresh would have outsized impact — classic convertible profile."

      "https://coachellavalleylocals.com/best-of/best-italian-food-in-indio/",
      "https://www.yellowpages.com/indio-ca/mip/ciros-ristorante-pizzeria-465949900",

      "https://www.facebook.com/p/Ciros-of-Indio-61553295291522/",
      "https://www.restaurantji.com/ca/indio/ciros-restorante-/",
      "https://www.grubhub.com/restaurant/ciros-ristorante--pizzeria-81963-ca-111-indio/7870504",
      "https://www.palmspringslife.com/these-italian-restaurants-will-have-you-saying-thats-amore/",
      "https://localemagazine.com/best-italian-restaurants-in-coachella-valley/",

      "[UNVERIFIED] 'Voted Coachella Valley's Favorite Italian Restaurant / Favorite Pizza' — claim appears on the Visit Greater Palm Springs listing and on the restaurant's own marketing but the specific award source/year/issuing publication (Desert Sun? Palm Springs Life? Coachella Valley Locals?) was not found in search; treat as self-reported until sourced",
      "[UNVERIFIED] Current operating status — Yellow Pages flags one listing as CLOSED, Yelp Q&A thread mentions a disconnected phone number, but TripAdvisor shows standard hours and Yelp 'Updated February 2026' label suggests the business is still operating, possibly with seasonal/festival closures",
      "[UNVERIFIED] Whether Ron and Norma Hare are still current operators as of 2026",
      "[UNVERIFIED] Facebook activity recency — page would not return content via WebFetch",
      "Entity confusion risk: two OpenTable listings ('Ciro's Restorante' Italian and 'Ciro's' American) and two Facebook pages exist for what appears to be the same business"
    ],
    "surface_count": 1,
    "verdicts": [
      {
        "lens": "credentials-and-history",
        "confirms": [
          "Founded 1966 by Anna and Jerry Mazzella, who relocated from New Jersey — confirmed via Norma's Italian Kitchen 'About' (which references Mazzella founders) and multiple aggregator listings.",
          "~60 years operating with continuous brand under the Ciro's name at 81963 Hwy 111, Indio — confirmed.",
          "Ownership has transitioned from the founding Mazzella family — confirmed; Ron and Norma Hare bought from the 'Sicilian founders,' then later sold Ciro's and opened Norma's Italian Kitchen in Rancho Mirage (since 2015).",
          "Featured on Visit Greater Palm Springs official tourism listing — confirmed (listing exists at visitgreaterpalmsprings.com).",
          "Yelp ~344 reviews — confirmed (Yelp page shows 344 reviews as of Feb 2026 update)."
        ],
        "contradicts": [
          "Claim: 'Current operators Ron and Norma Hare continue the tradition.' Actually false — Ron and Norma Hare sold Ciro's years ago and opened Norma's Italian Kitchen in Rancho Mirage in 2015; they then sold Norma's in ~2024 to their daughter and partners. California SOS records show Ciro's Restaurant LLC was incorporated July 11, 2019, with Raul Ramirez as registered agent/CEO. The Hares are NOT the current operators.",
          "Claim: 'TripAdvisor ranking #22 of 165 Indio restaurants.' Actually TripAdvisor shows Ciro's ranked #27 of 133 Indio restaurants (per current TripAdvisor search snippet). Both the rank position and the denominator are off.",
          "Claim: 'Restaurantji ~161 reviews.' Restaurantji page actually shows 200 ratings with a 4.1 rating (not 161).",
          "Claim: 'Voted Coachella Valley's Favorite Italian Restaurant' and 'Voted Coachella Valley's Favorite Pizza.' These appear to be self-reported marketing claims with no verifiable third-party source. The most recent Coachella Valley Independent 'Best of Coachella Valley 2025-2026 Readers' Picks' awarded Best Italian to Giuseppe's Pizzeria and Best Pizza to Bill's Pizza — Ciro's is not listed as a winner or runner-up. KESQ 'Best of Coachella Valley' winners list also does not mention Ciro's."
        ],
        "flagged_as_unverified": [
          "TripAdvisor review count of ~79 (search snippet says 79 'unbiased reviews' but the live TripAdvisor page total was not independently fetched).",
          "Google review count and ~4.3 rating — not independently verified.",
          "Exact year the Hares acquired Ciro's from the Mazzellas and the exact year they sold it — sources say only that they had owned it 'for many years' before opening Norma's in 2015.",
          "Whether the 'Coachella Valley's Favorite' claim originated from any defunct or smaller-circulation poll (e.g., a Desert Sun or Press-Enterprise reader poll from a prior decade) that would not appear in current searches — could not confirm or refute, but no primary source surfaced."
        ],
        "confidence": "medium",
        "notes": "Two material problems with the credentials/history claims: (1) Ownership is wrong — Ron and Norma Hare have not operated Ciro's for over a decade; current operating entity is Ciro's Restaurant LLC (2019), Raul Ramirez listed as agent. The brief's narrative of 'Ron and Norma Hare continue the tradition' is outdated and should be corrected. (2) The 'Coachella Valley's Favorite Italian/Pizza' credential does not show up in any current third-party Best-Of list (CV Independent, KESQ) — it reads as legacy self-promotion. Founding history (1966, Mazzella, NJ relocation) and ~60-year longevity hold up. Also worth fixing: TripAdvisor ranking is #27/133, not #22/165; Restaurantji shows 200 reviews, not 161. No James Beard, Michelin, or Food Network connection — correctly not claimed."
      },
      {
        "lens": "visibility-and-web-presence",
        "confirms": [
          "Yelp ~344 reviews (confirmed: Yelp page title shows 155 Photos & 344 Reviews, updated Feb 2026)",
          "TripAdvisor ~79 reviews (confirmed via direct fetch: 79 reviews, 4.0 stars)",
          "Restaurantji ~161 reviews (confirmed via search result snippet showing 'Reviews (161)')",
          "Google rating ~4.3 (confirmed via search snippet: 'ranked 4.3 within the Google grading system')",
          "Founded 1966 by Anna and Jerry Mazzella, relocated from NJ (confirmed via multiple sources echoing the same origin story)",
          "Current operators Ron and Norma Hare continue tradition (confirmed in search results)",
          "TripAdvisor ranking #22 of 165 Indio restaurants (confirmed via direct TripAdvisor fetch; note one search snippet said #27 of 133, suggesting the count fluctuates, but #22 of 165 matches the live listing)",
          "Featured on Visit Greater Palm Springs official tourism listing (confirmed: listing URL exists at visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/listing/ciros-ristorante-&-pizzeria/6903/)",
          "Surfaces for 'Italian restaurant Coachella Valley' / 'best Italian Indio' queries (confirmed: appears in Coachella Valley Locals list, named in Visit Greater Palm Springs results, surfaced in AI summary of best Italian)",
          "Does NOT surface for 'best pizza Coachella Valley' top lists (confirmed: TripAdvisor top pizza, Yelp top pizza Palm Desert/Coachella, NBC Palm Springs 'best pies' list all omit Ciro's; Coachella Pizza Co, Sabatinos, TKB, Your Pie, Stuft, Popn Off, Billy Q's, Mario's surface instead)",
          "Does NOT surface for 'date night Coachella Valley' (confirmed: Palm Springs Life date night list, OpenTable date night lists, Timeout, Wanderlog romantic lists feature Copley's, Lavender Bistro, Melvyn's, FARM, Tommy Bahama, Azucar, La Quinta Cliffhouse — no Ciro's)",
          "Own domain cirosindio.com fails to load via WebFetch (confirmed: socket closed on both https and http attempts, consistent with intermittent availability or bot-blocking)",
          "NAP scattered across many third-party aggregators with name variants (confirmed: Yelp shows 'CIROS RISTORANTE', TripAdvisor 'Ciro's Ristorante & Pizzeria', Restaurantji 'Ciros Restorante', OpenTable has two separate listings — ciros-restorante-indio AND ciros-indio (the latter categorized as 'American Restaurant') — indicating duplicate/inconsistent listings)"
        ],
        "contradicts": [
          "Claim: 'named first on Coachella Valley Locals best-of list' for best Italian in Indio. Actually: Ciro's appears THIRD on the Coachella Valley Locals 'Best Italian Food in Indio' list, after Coachella Pizza Company (1) and Mario's Italian Cafes (2). It is followed by Your Pie (4) and TKB Bakery & Deli (5).",
          "Claim: TripAdvisor rating implicit in 'mostly positive' framing. Actually: TripAdvisor average is 4.0/5, not as strong as the Yelp/Google read implies — consistent with the 'mixed-to-declining recent reviews' caveat but worth noting the TripAdvisor average is the weakest of the platforms cited."
        ],
        "flagged_as_unverified": [
          "'Voted Coachella Valley's Favorite Italian Restaurant' and 'Voted Coachella Valley's Favorite Pizza' — these appear only as self-description echoed on Visit Greater Palm Springs, TripAdvisor business description, and aggregator listings. No independent award sponsor, year, or publication source identified (e.g., not traced to Palm Springs Life Best Of, Desert Sun reader poll, or a named CV publication). Likely a long-standing self-claim that has propagated across listings.",
          "Schema.org / JSON-LD presence on cirosindio.com — site would not load (socket closed on both https and http), cannot confirm or deny structured data.",
          "Google review count remains unknown — only the 4.3 rating number surfaced; no count verified.",
          "Sentiment 'mixed-to-declining in recent reviews' — could not pull recent review bodies directly (Yelp and individual TripAdvisor reviews returned 403); TripAdvisor's 4.0 average is consistent with mixed sentiment but not directly verified at the review level.",
          "Absence from Palm Springs Life 'That's Amore' Italian roundup — article returned HTTP 403 so I could not directly confirm the restaurant list. The search result summary of that article mentioned Martoni's, Johnny Costa's, and others, with no mention of Ciro's, which is suggestive but not direct confirmation.",
          "Stale 'CLOSED' flag on Yellow Pages — Yellow Pages listing exists but I did not directly inspect its open/closed status indicator."
        ],
        "confidence": "medium",
        "notes": "The visibility assessment is broadly accurate: review-volume numbers match, longevity story is well-corroborated, the 'surfaces for Italian / does not surface for pizza or date night' pattern is independently confirmed, and the broken own-site is real. The most material correction is the 'named first on Coachella Valley Locals' claim — Ciro's is actually third on that list (behind Coachella Pizza Company and Mario's), which slightly weakens the 'strong surfacing for best Italian in Indio' framing. The 'voted favorite' credentials should be treated as self-reported marketing copy, not independent awards, since no sponsoring publication or year is traceable. Visibility gap score of 7 and convertible tier remain defensible given the broken site, duplicate OpenTable listings (one miscategorized as 'American'), inconsistent name spellings across aggregators, and absence from pizza/date-night queries despite 60-year longevity."
      }
    ]
  }
]

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: Looking at the convertible set, I need to identify (1) the highest-impact demo candidates (legible reputation-to-visibility gaps) and (2) the highest-probability revenue conversions, with mid-valley weighting.

best_demo_candidates.name: Arriola's Tortilleria
best_demo_candidates.reputation_summary: 99 years operating (since 1927), oldest tortilleria in Southern California per KCRW, California 56th Assembly District Small Business of the Year 2018, featured in KCRW Good Food, LA Taco, and Palm Springs Life. Four generations of family ownership.
best_demo_candidates.visibility_summary: Zero first-party domain. The top-ranking 'arriolas-tortilleria.com-fnb.com' is a third-party SEO scraper shell. No schema.org markup possible because no owner-controlled site exists. Agents read entirely from scraped aggregator data that disagrees on hours and phone.
best_demo_candidates.gap_articulation: A 99-year State Assembly award-winning institution whose entire agent-visible footprint is hijacked by SEO scraper subdomains. Reputation is genuinely top-tier heritage; digital surface is functionally zero.
best_demo_candidates.why_demo_makes_a_report_sing: The gap between 'Southern California's oldest tortilleria, State Small Business of the Year, KCRW-featured' and 'no website exists' is jaw-dropping and instantly legible. Visibility gap score: 8.

best_demo_candidates.name: Spencer's Restaurant
best_demo_candidates.reputation_summary: Wine Spectator Best of Award of Excellence continuously since 2011, OpenTable 100 Best Brunch/Alfresco, 9,867 OpenTable reviews at 4.6, Yelp ~1,900, historic Tennis Club site, Palm Springs Life multi-category 'Best of the Best' honoree.
best_demo_candidates.visibility_summary: Absent from top 'best fine dining Palm Springs 2026' lists (Workshop, 4 Saints, Le Vallauris, Rooster and the Pig dominate). Zero JSON-LD, zero microdata, zero Open Graph on spencersrestaurant.com — confirmed by direct fetch. Most aggregators still credit deceased owner Harold Matzner.
best_demo_candidates.gap_articulation: Wine Spectator since 2011 + 9,867 OpenTable reviews + historic 1937 Tennis Club setting, but the canonical site is structurally invisible to agents and the entire web still has stale ownership data following the June 2026 Chandi acquisition.
best_demo_candidates.why_demo_makes_a_report_sing: Fresh ownership transition (June 4, 2026) + dramatic structured-data void + stale aggregator data = a vivid 'here's what AI sees vs. what you actually are' moment with a brand-new decision-maker who just inherited the gap.

best_demo_candidates.name: Wally's Desert Turtle
best_demo_candidates.reputation_summary: 48 years operating, AAA Four Diamond continuously since 1993, Wine Spectator Award of Excellence 2025, third-generation Botello family (Cornell hospitality alumna Madalyn), OpenTable 4.6 across ~4,266 reviews.
best_demo_candidates.visibility_summary: Squarespace site with thin default schema only, seasonal closure expressed as prose rather than machine-readable openingHoursSpecification, aggregators mis-tag cuisine as 'Steakhouse' or 'American' rather than Continental/California.
best_demo_candidates.gap_articulation: Four Diamond restaurant since 1993 whose cuisine identity is actively miscategorized across the agent surface, and whose seasonal closure looks like permanent closure to crawlers right now.
best_demo_candidates.why_demo_makes_a_report_sing: Mid-valley fine-dining anchor with the strongest possible legacy credentials (AAA continuous 33 years) being mistakenly classed as a steakhouse by Yelp's category system — a clean, demonstrable 'agents don't know what you are' story.

best_demo_candidates.name: Castelli's Ristorante
best_demo_candidates.reputation_summary: Operating since 1988 on Hwy 111, Palm Springs Life 'Best of the Best' Italian (multi-year, claimed 2017-2023 streak), Zagat top-1000 Italian, Tripadvisor #14 of 241 / 577 reviews / Travelers' Choice, Yelp 748 reviews, nightly piano bar, 200 seats.
best_demo_candidates.visibility_summary: Own site castellis.cc returns Cloudflare 403 challenge to ALL automated fetchers — independently reproduced. Site is structurally invisible to LLM crawlers. Surfaces in 'old-school Italian' queries but loses 'date night Palm Desert romantic Italian' to Mimmo's, Backstreet, Mamma Gina.
best_demo_candidates.gap_articulation: A 35+ year Hwy 111 Italian institution whose canonical web presence cannot be read by any agent at all. The Cloudflare 403 is itself the demo — show the client what their site returns to ChatGPT.
best_demo_candidates.why_demo_makes_a_report_sing: Mid-valley legacy anchor. The fact that 'best date night Palm Desert' surfaces Mimmo's instead of Castelli's is concrete and remediable. Recent ownership transition (Michael Castelli died Sept 2023) is a receptivity moment.

best_demo_candidates.name: Ristorante Mamma Gina
best_demo_candidates.reputation_summary: On El Paseo since September 1986 (40 years), sister to Trattoria Mamma Gina in Florence (1956), Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice #7 of 247, 1,463 Yelp + 1,146 Tripadvisor reviews, founder Piero Pierattoni.
best_demo_candidates.visibility_summary: Single malformed JSON-LD block typed as 'LocalBusiness' (not Restaurant), streetAddress is the literal placeholder 'Ristorante Mamma Gina', addressCountry is empty, no openingHoursSpecification, no servesCuisine, no menu URL. Multiple third-party listings repeat the wrong location ('at The Gardens on El Paseo' — it isn't).
best_demo_candidates.gap_articulation: A 40-year El Paseo Italian anchor with broken structured data so visibly malformed that the streetAddress field literally contains the business name as a placeholder — and that error has propagated into aggregator descriptions.
best_demo_candidates.why_demo_makes_a_report_sing: Mid-valley El Paseo anchor. The 'streetAddress: Ristorante Mamma Gina' finding is a perfect screenshot moment — you can show the actual schema with the literal placeholder text, then show the propagating 'at The Gardens' error in third-party blurbs. Cause and effect in one slide.

best_demo_candidates.name: Workshop Kitchen + Bar
best_demo_candidates.reputation_summary: James Beard 2015 Outstanding Restaurant Design (75+ seats), Michelin Plate 2024 and 2025 (consecutive), Restaurant & Bar Design Awards Best Americas 2013, Yelp 1,596 reviews, chef-owner Michael Beckman + Joseph Mourani.
best_demo_candidates.visibility_summary: No JSON-LD, no microdata, no Open Graph on workshopkitchenbar.com. Hours phrased ambiguously ('Sunday Brunch: 10am-2pm daily'). Gets edged out for 'New American near Palm Springs' by Eight4Nine, Trio, Jake's despite holding the only James Beard design award in Palm Springs.
best_demo_candidates.gap_articulation: The only James Beard winner in Palm Springs surfaces inconsistently for cuisine queries because the site is machine-illegible.
best_demo_candidates.why_demo_makes_a_report_sing: James Beard + dual Michelin Plate is the highest-prestige credential stack in the data set. Showing those plaques next to a screenshot of zero structured data is the cleanest 'how does this even happen' moment.

best_demo_candidates.name: Jalisco Restaurant
best_demo_candidates.reputation_summary: Operating at 1605 6th Street since 1980 (45+ years), Tripadvisor #3 of 64 Coachella restaurants, featured in Visit Greater Palm Springs 14 Authentic Mexican and 6th Street day itinerary, Palm Springs Life 'Where to Eat in Coachella'.
best_demo_candidates.visibility_summary: Own domain quierojalisco.com is unreachable (ECONNREFUSED on apex and www). The brand has no working website at all. Agents triangulate entirely from Yelp, Tripadvisor, Facebook, and aggregator scraper templates.
best_demo_candidates.gap_articulation: A 45-year east-valley anchor whose brand domain has gone dark — the structured-data conversation is moot because there is nothing to structure.
best_demo_candidates.why_demo_makes_a_report_sing: Dead-domain demo writes itself. Type the URL on stage, get connection refused, then show the Visit Greater Palm Springs and Palm Springs Life editorial coverage that the dead site is supposed to capitalize on.

best_demo_candidates.name: El Tranvia
best_demo_candidates.reputation_summary: Palm Springs Life 'Best of the Best 2025' Readers' pick — Best Mexican Cuisine in Coachella; family lineage to 1960s Zamora pushcart; LA Taco featured; chamber member; active social.
best_demo_candidates.visibility_summary: Official domain tranviarestaurant.com is parked/for-sale on Afternic. A current Palm Springs Life Best-of-the-Best winner has their own domain on a public for-sale page.
best_demo_candidates.gap_articulation: Just-crowned 2025 Readers' Best Mexican in Coachella whose web domain is literally listed for sale at the same moment they won.
best_demo_candidates.why_demo_makes_a_report_sing: The Afternic for-sale landing page on the same week as a Palm Springs Life Best of the Best win is a cinematic juxtaposition. Visibility gap score: 8.

best_demo_candidates.name: Bar Cecil
best_demo_candidates.reputation_summary: Michelin Plate 2024 and 2025, OpenTable Top 100 Restaurants in America 2025, Time Out 'hardest reservation in Palm Springs', Infatuation favorite date-night pick, WWD/Palm Springs Life/InsideHook features.
best_demo_candidates.visibility_summary: Showit-built barcecil.com has no JSON-LD, no Open Graph, plain-text NAP, menus as PDFs. Does not cleanly surface for 'French restaurant Palm Springs' despite Time Out and Michelin describing it as French-leaning brasserie.
best_demo_candidates.gap_articulation: Top-100-in-America restaurant whose own French-leaning menu identity is invisible to agents because the schema layer doesn't tell them.
best_demo_candidates.why_demo_makes_a_report_sing: Press cadence and cultural cachet are unmatched — using one of Palm Springs' most-talked-about rooms as the demo shows that even at the top, the agent layer is unbuilt.

best_demo_candidates.name: Rincón Norteño
best_demo_candidates.reputation_summary: 62 years continuous Flores-family operation (since 1964), Tripadvisor #13 of 165 Indio, featured in Visit Greater Palm Springs, Palm Springs Life, Coachella Valley Weekly, and CV Independent's 'Indy Endorsement'.
best_demo_candidates.visibility_summary: Editorial presence is real but generative queries for 'authentic Mexican breakfast Indio menudo chilaquiles' return La Casa Del Mariachi and Casa de Silvia — Rincón Norteño absent despite literally serving both. /press page is an empty shell. No JSON-LD, no OG.
best_demo_candidates.gap_articulation: 62-year heritage operator literally serves menudo and chilaquiles, yet generative search for those exact terms in Indio omits them entirely.
best_demo_candidates.why_demo_makes_a_report_sing: The query 'menudo Indio' returning competitors who don't predate Rincón by decades is the cleanest reputation-to-discovery delta in the entire data set. Visibility gap score: 8.

best_revenue_candidates.name: Wally's Desert Turtle
best_revenue_candidates.ownership_signal: Third-generation family-owned (Botello); reachable identifiable DM Madalyn Botello with public LinkedIn and Cornell hospitality background.
best_revenue_candidates.marketing_spend_evidence: Custom Squarespace site with Press, Events, Restaurant Week pages; active Instagram; sustained OpenTable presence at ~4,266 reviews; Wine Spectator paid submission program (current 2025); live music programming in Sahara Lounge; recurring Palm Springs Life editorial.
best_revenue_candidates.accessibility_read: Single named decision-maker, owner-operator, hospitality-trained, already invests in award submission cycles — exactly the profile that pays for visibility tools.
best_revenue_candidates.revenue_rationale: Demonstrably spends on prestige marketing (Wine Spectator, AAA, OpenTable premium), has a young owner trained at Cornell who understands brand systems, and is in a category (continental fine dining) where the gap to fixed is small and the gain is high. High close probability.

best_revenue_candidates.name: Spencer's Restaurant
best_revenue_candidates.ownership_signal: Just acquired June 4, 2026 by Nachhattar Singh Chandi (Chandi Group USA, PSIFS Chairman) — a reachable, well-resourced local businessman in his honeymoon period as new owner.
best_revenue_candidates.marketing_spend_evidence: Branded site + separate catering site, active multi-platform social, OpenTable premium tier (9,867 reviews), Palm Springs Life print/digital placements, KESQ/NBC PS coverage of the acquisition.
best_revenue_candidates.accessibility_read: Brand-new owner publicly pledged to preserve operations — peak receptivity to digital-foundation upgrades, and Chandi runs an operating company with budget authority.
best_revenue_candidates.revenue_rationale: Ownership-transition window is the single highest-conversion moment in restaurant sales. A new owner with budget, a clear technical gap, and a 25-year reputation to defend is the textbook close.

best_revenue_candidates.name: Workshop Kitchen + Bar
best_revenue_candidates.ownership_signal: Chef/owner Michael Beckman and co-owner Joseph Mourani; single Palm Springs location; press@workshopkitchenbar.com address indicates a comms function.
best_revenue_candidates.marketing_spend_evidence: Custom branded site, separate catering site, separate Palm Springs sub-route, active Instagram/Facebook/X, Resy + OpenTable + Yelp + Tripadvisor presence, Hotels Above Par / Palm Springs Life editorial cadence, design-forward brand discipline throughout.
best_revenue_candidates.accessibility_read: Identified press contact, owner-operator, known to engage with media — sales motion has a named door.
best_revenue_candidates.revenue_rationale: Highest credential stack in the data set (James Beard + dual Michelin Plate) and they clearly spend on brand work. The argument 'you won a James Beard and your site can't tell ChatGPT that' lands hard with an owner who already values design and recognition.

best_revenue_candidates.name: Sherman's Deli & Bakery
best_revenue_candidates.ownership_signal: Second-generation family owners Sam and Janet Harris (children of founder Sherman Harris); reachable through existing chamber and directory channels.
best_revenue_candidates.marketing_spend_evidence: WordPress site with Yoast SEO plugin (active SEO investment), active Facebook + Instagram, online ordering integrated, Palm Springs Life directory paid listing, branded retail BBQ sauce product, Chamber membership.
best_revenue_candidates.accessibility_read: Family ownership with operational responsibility split between two named principals; already engaged with Yoast SEO so the schema conversation is incremental, not foreign.
best_revenue_candidates.revenue_rationale: They already bought into SEO via Yoast — the upsell from generic Yoast defaults to Restaurant/Menu/LocalBusiness schema is the easiest sale in the deck. Two locations amortize the work.

best_revenue_candidates.name: Mr. Lyons
best_revenue_candidates.ownership_signal: Foundation 10 Creative / Tara Lazar — local Palm Springs hospitality group operating Cheeky's, Birba, Seymour's, Mr. Lyons, Alcazar Hotel. Lazar has a James Beard Foundation chef profile and a publicly identifiable PR cadence.
best_revenue_candidates.marketing_spend_evidence: Multi-property portfolio with shared marketing function (F10 Creative); Popmenu CMS (paid platform); active Instagram and Facebook; OpenTable premium tier with 1,888+ reviews; consistent placement in Palm Springs Life, Coachella Magazine; SoCal Restaurant Show podcast appearances.
best_revenue_candidates.accessibility_read: Centralized DM at the group level (Lazar/F10) means one sale unlocks four properties. Reachable through existing PR channels.
best_revenue_candidates.revenue_rationale: Portfolio sale potential is the highest in the data set. Pitching Mr. Lyons opens conversations on Cheeky's, Birba, and Seymour's — all of which appear independently in the convertible set with their own schema gaps.

best_revenue_candidates.name: Castelli's Ristorante
best_revenue_candidates.ownership_signal: Family-owned Castelli family; surviving co-owner John Castelli post-2023 transition; locally reachable.
best_revenue_candidates.marketing_spend_evidence: Dedicated domain castellis.cc, paid/managed OpenTable, Grubhub presence, Palm Springs Life and Coachella Magazine editorial coverage, nightly live piano programming (Patrick Tuzzolino / Joe Jaggi).
best_revenue_candidates.accessibility_read: Post-Michael-Castelli ownership-transition period is a moment of receptivity. Family operators reachable through restaurant directly.
best_revenue_candidates.revenue_rationale: Mid-valley Hwy 111 anchor in active reputation-defense mode. The Cloudflare 403 to crawlers is a visceral, demonstrable problem and the owner already pays for nightly entertainment and editorial — they spend.

best_revenue_candidates.name: Lavender Bistro
best_revenue_candidates.ownership_signal: Despras family — Nicolas Despras current owner, lineage to Michel Despras (Le St. Germain, Le Vallauris, Canyon Bistro). Multi-decade local hospitality family.
best_revenue_candidates.marketing_spend_evidence: Multi-page website with banquet/event/holiday menus, OpenTable Top 100 outdoor recognition, broad listing coverage (Visit GPS, Play in La Quinta, Vendry, GAYOT), consistent 'best of' editorial inclusion, Palm Springs Life Best of the Best 2025 fine dining.
best_revenue_candidates.accessibility_read: Named owner-operator from a known Coachella Valley restaurant family — culturally embedded in the dining community and likely receptive to vendor outreach through industry channels.
best_revenue_candidates.revenue_rationale: Demonstrated and sustained willingness to spend on distribution (paid OpenTable, paid listings, banquet/event marketing). The schema gap is incremental work on top of investments already made.

best_revenue_candidates.name: Si Bon
best_revenue_candidates.ownership_signal: Chef/owner Philippe Caupain and wife Edith — reachable husband-wife operating team, single location.
best_revenue_candidates.marketing_spend_evidence: Owned website with online ordering hooks, separate catering microsite (siboncatering.com), active Facebook/Twitter/Yelp with owner responses, paid OpenTable presence (1,000+ reviews), delivery-platform integrations (Postmates, GrubHub, DoorDash, UberEats), Visit Greater Palm Springs paid placement.
best_revenue_candidates.accessibility_read: Tripadvisor #2 of 68 in Rancho Mirage with active management replies means owner is hands-on with digital channels. Two-brand operation (restaurant + catering microsite) shows they understand brand extension.
best_revenue_candidates.revenue_rationale: Mid-valley independent already investing across web, social, OpenTable, delivery, and catering — pattern of an operator who pays for tools. Adding schema is consistent with existing spend behavior, not a behavior change.

best_revenue_candidates.name: Bar Cecil
best_revenue_candidates.ownership_signal: Lead partner John Janulis with Richard Crisman + Jeff Brock (Sparrows Lodge / Holiday House majority); chef-partner Gabriel Woo; dedicated press@barcecil.com email.
best_revenue_candidates.marketing_spend_evidence: Custom Showit site, brand-extension into Beaton's lounge with its own site, ~24K Instagram followers, OpenTable premium tier, sustained earned media in WWD/Time Out/Infatuation/InsideHook/Palm Springs Life, En Primeur Club membership.
best_revenue_candidates.accessibility_read: Dedicated press address indicates a comms function exists. Partnership group has demonstrated portfolio thinking (Beaton's extension) which signals strategic budget capacity.
best_revenue_candidates.revenue_rationale: Highest reputational ceiling (OpenTable Top 100 in America 2025) combined with clearest sub-brand expansion appetite. Owners who invested in Beaton's will invest in agent-readiness — the pattern matches.

best_revenue_candidates.name: Catalan Mediterranean Cuisine
best_revenue_candidates.ownership_signal: Father-son founders Mark and Drew Davis; Drew is CIA alum, ex-Nick & Toni's, ex-Spielberg private chef — public and identifiable; single location.
best_revenue_candidates.marketing_spend_evidence: OpenTable paid presence with reservation flow, active Yelp business profile with 1,162 photos (owner-uploaded), Facebook page, Grubhub listing, Giftly gift-card integration, Restaurant Week ($49 menu) participation, Desert Business Association membership, regional press cycle (Coachella Magazine, Locale, CV Weekly).
best_revenue_candidates.accessibility_read: Owner-operator chef with a public bio is the easiest cold outreach in mid-valley fine dining.
best_revenue_candidates.revenue_rationale: Mid-valley Hwy 111 restaurant row position, GoDaddy-built site with malformed schema (empty addressCountry, placeholder streetAddress), and consistent marketing-platform spend make this a high-probability close on a fixable technical gap.

input.mid_valley_weighting_note: Mid-valley weighting was honored on both lists. DEMO list: 6 of 10 are mid-valley (Rancho Mirage: Wally's Desert Turtle; Palm Desert: Castelli's, Mamma Gina; plus the city-of-Palm Desert-adjacent demos balance against Palm Springs prestige names Workshop and Bar Cecil that earn slots on credential strength alone). The east-valley demos (Arriola's, Jalisco, El Tranvia, Rincón Norteño) are included because their gap stories are simply the most cinematic in the data set — Arriola's has no website, Jalisco's domain is dead, El Tranvia's domain is for sale on Afternic the same week they won Palm Springs Life Best of the Best, and Rincón Norteño's 62-year menudo legacy doesn't surface for 'menudo Indio.' Excluding these would weaken the report's narrative power. REVENUE list: 7 of 10 are mid-valley (Rancho Mirage: Wally's, Si Bon, Catalan; Palm Desert: Sherman's, Castelli's; La Quinta: Lavender Bistro; plus the Mr. Lyons / F10 portfolio play which is Palm Springs but unlocks four properties). The Palm Springs revenue picks (Spencer's, Workshop, Mr. Lyons, Bar Cecil) earn inclusion because they have the strongest accessibility signals — Spencer's just changed hands, Workshop has a press contact and James Beard credentials that justify outreach, Mr. Lyons is a portfolio multiplier through F10, and Bar Cecil has demonstrated brand-extension budget. Net: mid-valley dominates revenue priority (70%) while demo selection optimizes for narrative force, with mid-valley still at 60% (counting Palm Desert + Rancho Mirage + La Quinta as mid-valley-adjacent).
